lines.en_lit: Who is anonymous Sifra? Rabbi Yehuda. And teaches: The lottery makes it a sin-offering, the status does not make it a sin-offering. Apparently, the lottery is indispensable. refutation of the one who says that the lottery is not indispensable, is a conclusive refutation.
Rav Ḥisda said: Nests, become designated only Either upon the owner’s taking or upon the priest’s performance
Rav Shimi bar Ashi said: What is the reason of Rav Ḥisda? As it is written “And she shall take “And offer either upon taking or upon performance
raises an objection “And offer it for a sin-offering” the lottery makes it a sin-offering, the status does not make it a sin-offering.

as might Is there not an a fortiori inference: Just as in a case in which a lottery does not consecrate the required status does consecrate them, a case in which a lottery does consecrate is it not right that the required status should consecrate them?
the verse states: “He shall make it a sin-offering” the lottery makes it a sin-offering, but the status does not make it a sin-offering.
But here, which is neither the time of taking nor the time of performing teaches establish

Rava said: This is what is saying: Just as in a case in which a lottery does not consecrate even at the time of taking or at the time of performing the required status does consecrate them at the time of taking or at the time of performing a case in which a lottery does consecrate neither at the time of taking nor at the time of performing is it not right that the required status should consecrate them, at the time of taking or at the time of performing
the verse states: “He shall make it a sin-offering” the lottery makes it a sin-offering, but the status does not make it a sin-offering.

Come hear a poor person who ritually impurified the Temple, and he set aside money for his nests and he became wealthy, and afterward, said these are for his sin-offering and those are for his burnt-offering.
adds and brings his obligation from the money for his sin-offering, he may not add and bring his obligation from the money for his burnt-offering.
But here, which is neither the time of taking nor the time of performing And teaches establish

Rav Sheshet said: But can you understand Didn’t Rabbi Elazar say Rabbi Hoshaya said: A wealthy person who ritually impurifies the Temple and brings offering a poor does not fulfill Since he does not fulfill how establish
Rather, what have you to say That he had already said while in his impoverished state. Here also, that he had already said at the time of setting aside
But according to Rabbi Ḥaga, said Rabbi Yoshiya said does fulfill
